message:
feat(server): apply redis.conf directives at startup (frankenredis-5ney)

Closes the parity gap where fr-server accepted --config but only
recorded the path for CONFIG REWRITE. Now the file is parsed via
fr_config::parse_redis_config_bytes on startup and a curated subset
of directives (bind / port / requirepass / masteruser / masterauth
/ replicaof) takes effect before the runtime starts accepting
connections.

crates/fr-server/src/main.rs:

- usage() string learns a --config line describing the new dual
  role (startup directives + CONFIG REWRITE target).
- New StartupConfig struct holding Option<Option<...>> for each
  supported directive so "set to empty" and "not specified" are
  distinguishable - users can now turn requirepass off by writing
  `requirepass ""` in the file without losing any CLI semantics.
- New load_startup_config_file(path) that reads the file via
  std::fs::read + parse_redis_config_bytes, walks the resulting
  ParsedConfigDirective list, and fills in the StartupConfig
  fields. Returns Err with a clean "failed to read" / "failed to
  parse" message the caller can print before exiting.
- main() applies the loaded StartupConfig after the CLI arguments
  have been parsed: CLI wins on conflict (so --port on the
  command line still overrides a port directive in the file),
  but anything the CLI left unset falls through to the file
  value. This mirrors how Redis's own loadServerConfigFromString
  layers over the CLI.
- requirepass / masteruser / masterauth are stored into the
  runtime state via the existing config-set code path so INFO
  and CONFIG GET observe them immediately.
- replicaof triggers the same replication-start code path that
  --replicaof does, so `replicaof 127.0.0.1 6380` in redis.conf
  becomes a real replica handshake at boot.

crates/fr-server/tests/tcp_e2e_test.rs:

- New spawn_frankenredis_config_only(port, config_path) helper
  that starts the server with only --mode strict + --config and
  waits for the TCP port to come up. Exercises the config-file
  boot path without additional CLI args so the regression test
  proves that directives actually apply - not just that they
  parse.
- New test asserts the boot sequence picks up bind / port /
  requirepass from a temp config file and that AUTH against the
  configured password succeeds, while AUTH with a wrong password
  is rejected.
